title: HMM Sequence Alignment and Profile Inference
source_data_quality_note: Source MD is a widefield-fluorescence fan-out artifact; principle grounded in domain literature.

E.description: HMM Sequence Alignment and Profile Inference: Profile HMM: learn emission and transition probabilities from multiple sequence alignment; find optimal alignment via Viterbi. The forward operator produces the measurement through a 3-node primitive DAG (M.hmm.profile_model…); recovery is posed as a statistical_inverse problem. Difficulty tier delta=5 with effective condition number kappa_eff~500; sequence_divergence_too_high, domain_boundaries_unknown set the accuracy floor at the Omega boundary. See the forward_model field for the closed-form equation.
E.forward_model: P(seq | HMM) = prod_t P(obs_t | state_t) * P(state_t | state_{t-1}); Viterbi decodes optimal path
E.world_state_x: HMM parameters: emission matrix E (20 amino acids x M_match states), transition matrix T
E.observation_y: multiple sequence alignment (N_seq sequences, L_length positions) for training

W.regime: Existence of the recovered HMM_profile_parameter_matrix is guaranteed within the declared Omega bounds. Uniqueness holds on the measurement-supported subspace; out-of-support modes are controlled by declared priors. Stability is conditionally stable (kappa_eff ~= 500); sequence_divergence_too_high dominates the stability cliff; the remaining mismatch parameters contribute higher-order bias terms. Multinomial sets the irreducible data-fidelity floor.

C.error_bound: Data-fidelity bound: alignment sensitivity > 80% at 25% sequence identity. Noise floor: O(sigma / sqrt(kappa_eff)) with kappa_eff~500. Mismatch-induced bias + C2 * |sequence_divergence_too_high|; iterative solvers achieve q=2 convergence (O(1/k^2)) near the optimum; epsilon_fn captures quality degradation across declared Omega.
C.complexity: O(N_seq * L ** 2 * M) for Viterbi + Baum-Welch per iteration
